title: Single-Cell and Spatial Transcriptomic Profiling Reveals Mechanisms of Angiogenesis and Fibrosis in Adenomyosis
author: Duan, Maoteng
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This dataset originates from a multi-omics study of  adenomyosis , a common gynecologic disorder characterized by limited therapeutic options and poorly understood molecular mechanisms. Using  single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq)  and  spatial transcriptomics (ST) , we generated a comprehensive cellular and spatial atlas of adenomyosis and further integrated publicly available  bulk RNA-seq datasets  for comparative analysis.
steps_raw: This dataset originates from a multi-omics study of  adenomyosis , a common gynecologic disorder characterized by limited therapeutic options and poorly understood molecular mechanisms. Using  single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq)  and  spatial transcriptomics (ST) , we generated a comprehensive cellular and spatial atlas of adenomyosis and further integrated publicly available  bulk RNA-seq datasets  for comparative analysis.
